contract_authority.semantic_rule: GNHF section ownership is based on workflow family, not solely on whether the payload begins with the gnhf executable.

section.placement: after the standard-AI advanced extension block

prompts.sprint_path_role: Compile operator intent into one versioned GNHF prompt result
prompts.use_this_when: A repo-aware AI has a regular request and must produce one bounded GNHF prompt without starting the sprint.
prompts.inspect_first: AgentSwitchboard regular-sprint-request v1, compiled-gnhf-prompt-result v1, repo rules, Git evidence, validators, artifacts, agent spawn evidence, and operator constraints.
prompts.expected_output: Exactly one compiled-gnhf-prompt-result v1 JSON document containing one self-contained GNHF prompt.
prompts.next_step: Validate the JSON with AgentSwitchboard, then choose a separate launch surface if execution is intended.
prompts.proof_gate: The v1 result validates, selects exactly one Git mode, contains all bounds and proof fields, and no execution begins.
lines: COMPILE ONLY. DO NOT EXECUTE THE SPRINT, START GNHF, MODIFY FILES, OR CLAIM RUNTIME PROOF.
INPUT

Accept one AgentSwitchboard regular-sprint-request v1 object with kind regular-sprint-request, schemaVersion 1, objective, repository, ownedScope, forbiddenScope, expectedArtifacts, safetyConstraints, desiredProofLevel, executionIntent, readFirst, and validators.
executionIntent must be exactly one of compile_only, local_execute, environment_configure, or registered_workflow_execute. Compile-only requests must not authorize -Run.

Inspect repository evidence when available: AGENTS.md, Git status and branch, recent commits, worktrees, open PRs, plans, manifests, validators, tests, artifacts, logs, CI, and exact agent spawn evidence.
Mark stale or unavailable evidence. Do not invent repository facts or depend on unprovided chat history.
OUTPUT
Return exactly one JSON document conforming to AgentSwitchboard compiled-gnhf-prompt-result v1. Return no prose before or after it.
Set kind to compiled-gnhf-prompt-result and schemaVersion to 1.

Include repository, prompt, gitExecution, agentRoute, bounds, ownedScope, forbiddenScope, expectedArtifacts, readFirst, validationOrder, commitContract, pushContract, stopCondition, proofLevel, proofCeiling, finalResponseContract, and nextCommand.
Choose exactly one gitExecution.mode: worktree or current-branch. Never request both.
Bounds must include maxIterations, maxTokens, preventSleep, and timeoutSeconds.
Each expected artifact must use a repository-relative path and state its proof.
commitContract.required must be true and require a commit ahead of the base.
pushContract.mode must be none or manual; merge and deploy must be false.
The embedded prompt must instruct GNHF to inspect, modify tracked files, validate, commit, and stop with evidence.
A process exit, repeated stop text, unsupported adapter, authentication request, or quota exhaustion is not completion proof.
Reject secrets, credential-like values, automatic merge or deployment, default-branch writes, destructive cleanup, and personal or production data mutation.
The compiled prompt must be self-contained and paste-ready, but this compile-only prompt must not launch it.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Build or repair a repo-local AI harness and commit the implementation
prompts.use_this_when: A repository needs executable agent rules, workflows, registries, validators, skills, capabilities, triggers, and proof gates.
prompts.inspect_first: Existing repo rules, tests, scripts, manifests, hooks, generated-output policy, branch and PR conventions, and any partial harness.
prompts.expected_output: Committed harness spine and agent harness with deterministic routing, validators, fixtures, workflow contracts, documentation, and a clean handoff.
prompts.next_step: Use P47 to execute one committed harness workflow against a bounded product sprint.
prompts.proof_gate: A fresh agent can discover repo law, select a workflow, run validators, avoid known traps, register artifacts, and prove a commit ahead of base.
lines: gnhf `
--agent xyz_proven_gnhf_agent_spec `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 8 `
--max-tokens 800000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "The repo-local harness is implemented and committed, required validators pass or exact blockers are committed, generated artifacts are registered, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path
Sprint: Build or repair the repo-local AI harness.
Lane: harness spine, agent harness, integration seams, validation, and operator reporting.
Do not return a plan only. Inspect existing repository rules, tests, scripts, manifests, hooks, plans, validators, artifact policies, branch conventions, and partial harness files before inventing.

Build the smallest coherent harness that lets a fresh agent discover governing instructions, choose the correct workflow, call verified capabilities, respond to deterministic triggers, run validators, register outputs, avoid forbidden mutations, commit useful work, and hand off cleanly.

Implement schemas, fixtures, validators, skills, capabilities, triggers, workflow and artifact registries, run context, proof gates, and explicit integration seams as required by current repository evidence.
Keep product behavior in domain code and contracts rather than prompts. Reuse existing repo patterns and do not create a competing harness.
Validation order:
1. parse schemas, manifests, and scripts
2. run focused harness contract tests
3. run trigger and capability fixtures
4. run existing repository validators
5. run build or static checks
6. run secret, generated-output, and git-diff hygiene checks
Commit coherent tracked changes. Do not merge, force-push, write the default branch, expose secrets, delete unknown worktrees, or claim runtime proof from static checks.
A plan-only response is failure.
Final response must include branch, commit SHA, files, harness factoring, validations, artifacts, blockers, proof achieved, proof ceiling, final Git status, and one exact next command."

prompts.sprint_path_role: Execute one committed harness workflow with artifact and commit proof
prompts.use_this_when: The repo-local harness exists and one registered workflow should perform a bounded product or repair sprint.
prompts.inspect_first: Harness registries, selected workflow, run context, artifact contract, owned files, validators, current Git floor, and prior run evidence.
prompts.expected_output: One bounded workflow execution with registered artifacts, checkpoint commits, validation evidence, updated run context, and an exact blocker when completion is unsafe.
prompts.next_step: Review the artifact and commit proof, then push or update the PR only when authorized.
prompts.proof_gate: The workflow is registered, every completed item has artifact or code proof, canonical validators run, and the required commit exists ahead of base.
lines: gnhf `
--agent xyz_proven_gnhf_agent_spec `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 10 `
--max-tokens 1000000 `
--prevent-sleep on `

--stop-when "The selected registered workflow has completed its bounded queue or every remaining item has an exact committed blocker, required artifacts and validation evidence are registered, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path
Sprint: Execute one repo-local harness workflow.
Workflow ID: xyz_registered_workflow_id
Owned scope: xyz_owned_scope
Forbidden scope: xyz_forbidden_scope

Do not return a plan only. Read the repo law, run context schema, workflow registry, artifact registry, selected workflow, capability and trigger contracts, current branch and worktrees, recent commits, tests, validators, and prior evidence.
Refuse unregistered workflows, missing inputs, unknown partial work, absent mutation authority, or a failed exact agent spawn preflight.
Execute through repository code and contracts. Use checkpoint commits for coherent completed items and do not repeat an identical deterministic failure without a changed condition.
Register every required artifact with run ID, inputs and hashes where allowed, producing workflow, validator profile, privacy class, proof level, and proof ceiling.
Run workflow validation, repository validators, build checks, secret and generated-output hygiene, git diff --check, and final Git status.
Do not merge, force-push, deploy, release, mutate personal or production state, weaken validators, or hide failures.
A successful process exit without the required artifact and commit is failure.

Final response must include workflow ID, run ID, branch, checkpoint commits, files, artifacts, validations, blocked items, proof achieved, proof ceiling, final Git status, and one exact next command."

prompts.sprint_path_role: Generate, save, visibly print, and execute one compiled prompt locally
prompts.use_this_when: Cursor has granted repository and terminal access and the operator explicitly wants local GNHF execution.
prompts.inspect_first: Local repository and terminal access, AgentSwitchboard v1 schemas, Cursor runtime entrypoint, clean Git state, route readiness, validators, and intended PR authority.
prompts.expected_output: Local request and compiled-prompt evidence, visible full prompt emission, bounded GNHF start, required artifact and commit proof, validators, and exact proof ceiling.
prompts.next_step: Review the AgentSwitchboard GnhfCursor evidence directory and the pushed or updated PR when push authority was granted.
prompts.proof_gate: The Cursor agent initiated the chain, the complete prompt was printed visibly, GNHF or the disposable local harness started, and required artifacts and commits prove success.
lines: EXECUTE THIS LOCAL SPRINT IN CURSOR. DO NOT MERELY PRINT A COMMAND FOR THE OPERATOR.
1. Confirm this Cursor agent has the intended local repository and terminal access.
2. Inspect AGENTS.md, Git status and branch, recent commits, worktrees, PR evidence, validators, plans, artifacts, and current AgentSwitchboard v1 contract files.

3. Convert the request into regular-sprint-request v1 and compiled-gnhf-prompt-result v1 JSON with executionIntent local_execute, exactly one Git mode, and bounded iterations, tokens, timeout, and sleep prevention.
4. Save both documents to approved ignored local evidence paths. Do not put credentials, private data, or machine-local paths in tracked prompt source.
5. Invoke AgentSwitchboard tooling/gnhf/Invoke-CursorGnhfSprint.ps1 with -RequestPath, -CompiledPromptPath, -TargetRepo, and -Run. Use disposable proof only for a disposable repository.
6. Require the entrypoint to print the complete compiled prompt visibly before GNHF begins.
7. Treat spawn, authentication, quota, dirty-target, detached-target, or workstation-plan failures as exact blockers.
8. Require the contracted artifact and a commit ahead of base. Process exit alone is failure.
9. Run repository validation, inspect branch and worktree state, and preserve returned failed-work references when available.
10. Push or open or update the intended PR only when authorized; never force-push, merge, deploy, or write the default branch.
11. Report request and compiled-prompt paths, evidence directory, branch, worktree, commit SHA, artifacts, validation, blockers, proof, final Git state, and one next command.
Plan mode is the entrypoint default. This prompt requests local execution, so -Run must be explicit after contracts and clean-target gates pass.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Route workstation setup through AgentSwitchboard's Plan and Apply entrypoint
prompts.prompt_name: GNHF and Development Agent Environment Auto-Configurator
prompts.use_this_when: A local coding agent must configure or repair the workstation environment before a GNHF runtime sprint.
prompts.inspect_first: AgentSwitchboard workstation setup manifest and entrypoint, current installation inventory, unmanaged configuration, authentication state, reboot requirements, evidence root, and rollback paths.
prompts.expected_output: A reviewed Plan, an explicit idempotent Apply when authorized, preserved configuration, exact evidence and rollback paths, and honest authentication or reboot blockers.
prompts.next_step: Run P48 only after the environment Plan or authorized Apply and focused workstation contracts pass.
prompts.proof_gate: AgentSwitchboard repository-owned setup code produced Plan or Apply evidence; no prompt-embedded installer or automatic authentication was used.
lines: CONFIGURE THE LOCAL GNHF AND DEVELOPMENT AGENT ENVIRONMENT THROUGH AGENTSWITCHBOARD. DO NOT EMBED OR REIMPLEMENT INSTALLATION LOGIC IN THIS PROMPT.
1. Confirm local repository and terminal access and inspect AgentSwitchboard instructions, workstation manifest, setup scripts, tests, and installation evidence.
2. Invoke tooling/wsl/Start-TmuxGnhfWorkspaceSetup.ps1 in -Mode Plan first and save the exact Plan evidence path.
3. Inventory PowerShell 7, WSL Ubuntu, WezTerm, tmux, Node, GNHF, OpenCode, AGY, Goose, and other approved agents represented by AgentSwitchboard.
4. Reuse healthy installations and preserve unmanaged or user-owned configuration.
5. Do not automate provider authentication, OAuth, API keys, credential entry, or secret capture.
6. Identify reboot and resume checkpoints before Apply and record evidence, backup, rollback, and resume paths.
7. Run -Mode Apply only after explicit operator authorization. Apply must be idempotent and bounded.
8. Run AgentSwitchboard workstation contracts and classify unsupported tools, authentication, reboot, quota, and network conditions honestly.
9. Do not create a second installer, use focus-dependent automation, modify unrelated repositories, merge, deploy, or expose credentials.
10. Report mode, reused and changed components, evidence, backups, rollback, checkpoints, validation, blockers, proof, ceiling, and one next command.
